---
title: "Is Jonathan Tawn an Under-Achiever?"
date: Lancaster - 30 June, 2026
author: Stuart Coles
output: 
    beamer_presentation: 
       keep_tex: true
header-includes:
        - \usetheme[numbering = none]{metropolis}
        - \usepackage{xcolor}
        - \usepackage{amssymb}
        - \usepackage{bbm}
        - \usepackage{tikz}
        - \usepackage{fontawesome5}
        - \usetikzlibrary{positioning,arrows.meta,matrix}
        - \usetikzlibrary{calc}
        - \definecolor{mygray}{gray}{0.9}
fonttheme: "structurebold"
---

# Who do you think you are?

\pause

```{=latex}

\vspace {0.5cm}
\centering 
\input{lineage.tex}
```

# Early signs of a love of maths...

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center',  out.width="60%"}
knitr::include_graphics("pics/jpic1.JPG")
```


# Early signs of a dislike for travel...

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center',  out.width="60%"}
knitr::include_graphics("pics/jpic2.JPG")
```


# The sheriff...

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center',  out.width="60%"}
knitr::include_graphics("pics/jpic3.JPG")
```


# Academic highlights

* BSc Mathematics (First Class Honours), Imperial College, London, 1982--85

* Phd Statistics, University of Surrey, 1985--88

* Lecturer, University of Sheffield (1988–1992)

* Senior Lecturer, Lancaster University (1992–1996)

* Professor of Statistics, Lancaster University (1996–2017)

* Distinguished Professor of Statistics, Lancaster University (2017–present)

# The role of chance

\pause

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center',  out.width="60%"}
knitr::include_graphics("pics/jpic4.JPG")
```

# Awards

* RSS Guy Medal in Bronze (1993)

* RSS Barnett Award (2015, inaugural winner)

* RSS Guy Medal in Silver (2024)


# \begingroup\color[RGB]{255,215,0}\faMedal\endgroup\ New Award

\pause

\vspace{1cm}

* Most distinguished professor in the world not to own a mobile phone. (1991 - present)



# While I'm away for the next couple of weeks...

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center', out.width="50%"}
knitr::include_graphics("pics/StuTasks.pdf")
```

# Missing page of tasks for "next couple of weeks"

\pause

1. Prove Fermat's last theorem...

\pause

2. ... and the Riemann hypothesis.

\pause


3. Read Cox and Hinkley, Barndorff-Nielssen and Cox, Billingsley, 
Bickel and Doksum, Lehmann and Casella, Karlin and Taylor, Durett, Kallenberg, Gelman et al, Bernardo and Smith, Beirlant et al. 

\pause

4. Don't forget: avoid data for the moment. 

# A confession...

\pause

That couple of weeks, I *only* looked at data.

# An aside

While getting a list of book references to include above, ChatGPT gave me this insightful suggestion:

\pause
\vspace{1cm}
> "Modelling Extremal Events": by Coles - Jon Tawn's own book; readable but the math can get very dense. 


# A shameless plug...

Myself, Anthony and Miguel de Carvalho are currently working on a Second Edition of "Jon's book":

\vspace{1cm}
```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center',  out.width="30%"}
knitr::include_graphics("pics/ismev2a.pdf")
```

# Jon's philosophy as a supervisor

> "A PhD is a training for research"

\pause

His methods were also a lesson:

* Take time to research the scientific context to a problem, as well as the relevant statistics.

* Choose and design models with care, using science and mathematics as steers where available.

* Check the fit of models carefully.

* Use learnings from models to create a scientific narrative.

* Seek to push back the boundaries of knowledge.


# An Outline of EVT

```{r, echo=FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.align = 'center', out.width="80%"}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

set.seed(123)

n <- 60
u <- 1.7

dat <- data.frame(
    time = 1:n,
    value = cumsum(rnorm(n, 0, 0.15)) + rexp(n, 1)
)

dat$block <- rep(1:6, each = 10)

# block maxima
bm <- dat |>
    group_by(block) |>
    slice_max(value, n = 1) |>
    mutate(method = "Block maxima")

# threshold exceedances
pot <- dat |>
    filter(value > u) |>
    mutate(method = "POT / Point Process")

base <- bind_rows(
    dat |> mutate(method = "Block maxima"),
    dat |> mutate(method = "POT / Point Process")
)
ggplot(base, aes(time, value)) +

  # underlying process
  geom_line(
    colour = "#B8C2CC",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +

  # block boundaries
  geom_vline(
    data = data.frame(
      x = seq(10.5, 50.5, by = 10),
      method = "Block maxima"
    ),
    aes(xintercept = x),
    colour = "#D9DDE1",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    linetype = 2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  # threshold
  geom_hline(
    data = data.frame(
      method = "POT / Point Process",
      u = u
    ),
    aes(yintercept = u),
    colour = "#5B8E7D",
    linewidth = 0.8,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +

  # retained observations
  geom_point(
    data = bm,
    colour = "#2C7FB8",
    fill = "#2C7FB8",
    size = 2.3
  ) +

  geom_point(
    data = pot,
    colour = "#2C7FB8",
    fill = "#2C7FB8",
    size = 2.3
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~method, ncol = 1) +

  labs(
    title = "Modelling Extremes",
    subtitle = "Block maxima versus POT / point process",
    x = "Time",
    y = NULL
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 17) +

  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 22
    ),

    plot.subtitle = element_text(
      size = 14,
      colour = "grey35"
    ),

    strip.text = element_text(
      face = "bold",
      size = 15
    ),

    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),

    panel.spacing = unit(1.2, "lines"),

    axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )
```

# Extremes of two (or more) quantities

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center', out.width="80%"}

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(patchwork)

set.seed(123)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Simulated bivariate time series
# --------------------------------------------------

n_years <- 10
obs_per_year <- 20
n <- n_years * obs_per_year

dat <- data.frame(
  time = 1:n,
  year = rep(1:n_years, each = obs_per_year)
)

dat$x <- rgamma(n, shape = 2, scale = 1)
dat$y <- 0.7 * dat$x + rgamma(n, shape = 2, scale = 0.7)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Block maxima (marginal)
# --------------------------------------------------

bm_x <- dat |>
  group_by(year) |>
  slice_max(x, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup()

bm_y <- dat |>
  group_by(year) |>
  slice_max(y, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup()

bm <- bind_rows(
  bm_x |> mutate(series = "X"),
  bm_y |> mutate(series = "Y")
)

bm_long <- bm |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(x, y),
    names_to = "var",
    values_to = "value"
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Point process (joint extremes)
# --------------------------------------------------

u <- quantile(pmax(dat$x, dat$y), 0.90)

pp <- dat |>
  filter(pmax(x, y) > u)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Time series plot
# --------------------------------------------------

dat_long <- dat |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(x, y),
    names_to = "var",
    values_to = "value"
  )

p_time <- ggplot(dat_long, aes(time, value)) +

  geom_line(colour = "grey78", linewidth = 0.8) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = seq(obs_per_year + 0.5,
                     n - obs_per_year + 0.5,
                     by = obs_per_year),
    colour = "grey90",
    linetype = 2
  ) +

  geom_point(
    data = bm_long,
    colour = "#D55E00",
    size = 2
  ) +

  facet_wrap(~var, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +

  labs(
    title = "Block maxima (marginal approach)",
    subtitle = "One extreme per block per variable",
    x = "Time",
    y = NULL
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Point process plot
# --------------------------------------------------

p_space <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = x, y = y)) +

  annotate("rect",
           xmin = u, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = 0, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#56B4E9", alpha = 0.12) +

  annotate("rect",
           xmin = 0, xmax = u,
           ymin = u, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "#56B4E9", alpha = 0.12) +

  geom_point(colour = "grey80", size = 1.2) +

  geom_point(
    data = pp,
    colour = "#0072B2",
    size = 2
  ) +

  geom_vline(xintercept = u, colour = "#0072B2", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = u, colour = "#0072B2", linewidth = 0.7) +

  coord_equal() +

  labs(
    title = "Point process approach",
    subtitle = "All observations with max(X, Y) > u",
    x = expression(X),
    y = expression(Y)
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

# --------------------------------------------------
# Combine panels
# --------------------------------------------------

p_time + p_space +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1.2, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Bivariate extremes: block maxima vs point process",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold")
    )
  )
```


# 50+ PhD Theses Summarised 

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center', out.width="50%"}

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

set.seed(123)

n_years <- 10
obs_per_year <- 20
n <- n_years * obs_per_year

dat <- data.frame(
  time = 1:n,
  year = rep(1:n_years, each = obs_per_year)
)

dat$x <- rgamma(n, shape = 2, scale = 1)
dat$y <- 0.7 * dat$x + rgamma(n, shape = 2, scale = 0.7)

u  <- quantile(pmax(dat$x, dat$y), 0.90)
u2 <- quantile(pmax(dat$x, dat$y), 0.97)

pp <- dat %>% filter(pmax(x, y) > u)

lim <- max(dat$x, dat$y)
off <- 0.06 * lim

ggplot(dat, aes(x = x, y = y)) +

  # -----------------------------
  # FIT REGION: x > u or y > u
  # -----------------------------
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = u, xmax = lim,
           ymin = 0, ymax = lim,
           fill = "#2C7FB8", alpha = 0.06) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = 0, xmax = lim,
           ymin = u, ymax = lim,
           fill = "#2C7FB8", alpha = 0.06) +

  # -----------------------------
  # EXTRAPOLATION REGION: x > u' or y > u'
  # -----------------------------
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = u2, xmax = lim,
           ymin = 0, ymax = lim,
           fill = "#D95F02", alpha = 0.07) +
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = 0, xmax = lim,
           ymin = u2, ymax = lim,
           fill = "#D95F02", alpha = 0.07) +

  # -----------------------------
  # DATA + EXCEEDANCES
  # -----------------------------
  geom_point(colour = "grey80", size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(data = pp, colour = "#0072B2", size = 2) +

  # -----------------------------
  # THRESHOLD LINES
  # -----------------------------
  geom_vline(xintercept = u,  colour = "#2C7FB8", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = u,  colour = "#2C7FB8", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = u2, colour = "#D95F02", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = u2, colour = "#D95F02", linewidth = 0.7) +

  # -----------------------------
  # AXIS LABELS FOR u and u'
  # -----------------------------

  annotate("text",
           x = u,
           y = -off,
           label = "u",
           colour = "#2C7FB8",
           size = 5) +

  annotate("text",
           x = u2,
           y = -off,
           label = "u'",
           colour = "#D95F02",
           size = 5) +

  annotate("text",
           x = -off,
           y = u,
           label = "u",
           colour = "#2C7FB8",
           size = 5,
           angle = 90) +

  annotate("text",
           x = -off,
           y = u2,
           label = "u'",
           colour = "#D95F02",
           size = 5,
           angle = 90) +

  # -----------------------------
  # FINAL FORMATTING
  # -----------------------------
  coord_equal(
    xlim = c(0, lim),
    ylim = c(0, lim),
    clip = "off"
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Moving from blue to orange regions",
    subtitle = "Mathematical models to make the jump",
    x = expression(X),
    y = expression(Y)
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey92"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),

    # CRITICAL: make room for axis labels outside panel
    plot.margin = margin(20, 40, 20, 40)
  )
```
 
# Component-by-component maxima

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center', out.width="80%"}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

set.seed(123)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Simulated bivariate time series
# --------------------------------------------------

n_years <- 10
obs_per_year <- 20
n <- n_years * obs_per_year

dat <- data.frame(
  time = 1:n,
  year = rep(1:n_years, each = obs_per_year)
)

dat$x <- rgamma(n, shape = 2, scale = 1)
dat$y <- 0.7 * dat$x + rgamma(n, shape = 2, scale = 0.7)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Marginal block maxima
# --------------------------------------------------

bm_x <- dat |>
  group_by(year) |>
  slice_max(x, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  transmute(time, var = "x", value = x)

bm_y <- dat |>
  group_by(year) |>
  slice_max(y, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  transmute(time, var = "y", value = y)

bm_long <- bind_rows(bm_x, bm_y)

# --------------------------------------------------
# Long format for plotting
# --------------------------------------------------

dat_long <- dat |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(x, y),
    names_to = "var",
    values_to = "value"
  )

# --------------------------------------------------
# Plot
# --------------------------------------------------

ggplot(dat_long, aes(time, value)) +

  geom_line(
    colour = "grey75",
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = seq(
      obs_per_year + 0.5,
      n - obs_per_year + 0.5,
      by = obs_per_year
    ),
    colour = "grey90",
    linetype = 2
  ) +

  geom_point(
    data = bm_long,
    colour = "#D55E00",
    size = 2.5
  ) +

  facet_wrap(
    ~ var,
    ncol = 1,
    scales = "free_y",
    labeller = as_labeller(c(x = "X", y = "Y"))
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Block maxima (marginal approach)",
    subtitle = "One extreme per block per variable",
    x = "Time",
    y = NULL
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

# Early history

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center',  out.width="60%"}
knitr::include_graphics("pics/jpic1.JPG")
```

# Tawn's Spawn Process

```{r, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=4, out.width="80%"}
library(ggplot2)
tawn <- data.frame(
    year = c(1991, 1994, 1995, 1997, 2000, 2003, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2009,
             2010, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2026),
    n_students = c(1, 1, 2, 3, 1, 4, 1, 1, 5, 1,
                   1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 5, 2, 4, 1, 3, 6)
)

years_full <- 1991:2026

tawn_full <- data.frame(
    year = years_full,
    n_students = sapply(years_full, function(y) {
        if (y %in% tawn$year) {
            tawn$n_students[tawn$year == y]
        } else {
            0
        }
    })
)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

tawn_plot <- tawn_full |>
  mutate(highlight = year == 2026)

ggplot(tawn_plot, aes(x = year, y = n_students, fill = highlight)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(n_students > 0, n_students, "")),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "FALSE" = "#2C7FB8",  # original blue
      "TRUE"  = "#A6CEE3"   # lighter blue for 2026
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(1990, 2026, by = 5),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = 0:max(tawn_full$n_students),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Annual Jon Tawn PhD Output",
    subtitle = "Number of PhD students graduating each year",
    x = NULL,
    y = "PhD students"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

```


# Return levels

```{r, echo = FALSE, message=FALSE, warn = FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=4, out.width="80%"}

tawn <- data.frame(
    year = c(1991, 1994, 1995, 1997, 2000, 2003, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2009,
             2010, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021),
    n_students = c(1, 1, 2, 3, 1, 4, 1, 1, 5, 1,
                   1, 1, 2, 1, 2, 5, 2, 4, 1, 3)
)

years_full <- 1991:2021

tawn_full <- data.frame(
    year = years_full,
    n_students = sapply(years_full, function(y) {
        if (y %in% tawn$year) {
            tawn$n_students[tawn$year == y]
        } else {
            0
        }
    })
)

X <- tawn_full$n_students

library(ismev)

gev_fit <- gev.fit(X, show = FALSE)
# gev_fit


# Return periods

T <- seq(2, 50, by = 1)

mu <- gev_fit$mle[1]
sigma <- gev_fit$mle[2]
xi <- gev_fit$mle[3]

# Return level function
z_T <- if (abs(xi) > 1e-6) {
    mu + (sigma/xi) * ((-log(1 - 1/T))^(-xi) - 1)
} else {
    mu - sigma * log(-log(1 - 1/T))
}

library(ggplot2)

df_rl <- data.frame(
    T = T,
    z_T = z_T
)

df_obs <- data.frame(
    T = rep(1, length(X)),
    X = X
)

ggplot(df_rl, aes(T, z_T)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, colour = "#2C7FB8") +
    geom_hline(
        yintercept = max(X),
        colour = "red",
        linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    geom_point(
        data = df_obs,
        aes(T, X),
        size = 2,
        alpha = 0.7
    ) +
    labs(
        title = "PhD Student Numbers per Year",
        x = "Return period (years)",
        y = "Return level (students/year)"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
```

# Return levels

Once every 45 years we'd expect Jonathan to successfully supervise around 6.3 million PhD students.


# Poisson analysis

```{r, echo = FALSE, message=FALSE, warn = FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=4, out.width="80%"}

## -------------------------
## Data
## -------------------------
dat <- data.frame(
  year = 1991:2021,
  n_students = c(
    1,0,0,1,2,0,3,0,0,1,0,0,4,0,1,1,5,0,1,1,0,
    1,2,1,2,5,0,2,4,1,3
  )
)

dat$year_c <- dat$year - mean(dat$year)

## -------------------------
## Models
## -------------------------
fit_orig <- glm(
  n_students ~ year_c,
  family = poisson,
  data = dat
)

dat_aug <- rbind(
  dat[, c("year", "n_students")],
  data.frame(year = 2026, n_students = 6)
)

dat_aug$year_c <- dat_aug$year - mean(dat_aug$year)

fit_aug <- glm(
  n_students ~ year_c,
  family = poisson,
  data = dat_aug
)

## -------------------------
## Prediction grid
## -------------------------
newdat <- data.frame(
  year = seq(1991, 2026, length.out = 300)
)

## original model predictions + CI
newdat$year_c <- newdat$year - mean(dat$year)

p1 <- predict(fit_orig, newdata = newdat, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)

newdat$orig <- exp(p1$fit)
newdat$orig_lwr <- exp(p1$fit - 1.96 * p1$se.fit)
newdat$orig_upr <- exp(p1$fit + 1.96 * p1$se.fit)

## augmented model predictions (no CI shown for clarity)
newdat$year_c <- newdat$year - mean(dat_aug$year)

p2 <- predict(fit_aug, newdata = newdat, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)

newdat$aug <- exp(p2$fit)

## hypothetical point
extra <- data.frame(year = 2026, n_students = 6)

## -------------------------
## Plot
## -------------------------
ggplot() +

  ## confidence band (original model only)
  geom_ribbon(
    data = newdat,
    aes(x = year, ymin = orig_lwr, ymax = orig_upr),
    fill = "steelblue",
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +

  ## observed data
  geom_point(
    data = dat,
    aes(year, n_students),
    size = 2.6,
    colour = "black"
  ) +

  ## hypothetical point
  geom_point(
    data = extra,
    aes(year, n_students),
    size = 3.2,
    shape = 17,
    colour = "grey30"
  ) +

  ## original model
  geom_line(
    data = newdat,
    aes(year, orig, colour = "Original model"),
    linewidth = 1.3
  ) +

  ## augmented model
  geom_line(
    data = newdat,
    aes(year, aug, colour = "Including 2026 = 6"),
    linewidth = 1.3,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +

  scale_colour_manual(
    name = "Model",
    values = c(
      "Original model" = "steelblue4",
      "Including 2026 = 6" = "firebrick3"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Number of students"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +

  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "grey85"),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(colour = "black")
  )
```

# Let's extrapolate (don't try this at home, kids)

```{r, echo = FALSE, message=FALSE, warning = FALSE, fig.width=6, fig.height=4, out.width="80%"}
library(ggplot2)

## -------------------------
## Data + model
## -------------------------
dat <- data.frame(
  year = 1991:2021,
  n_students = c(
    1,0,0,1,2,0,3,0,0,1,0,0,4,0,1,1,5,0,1,1,0,
    1,2,1,2,5,0,2,4,1,3
  )
)

dat$year_c <- dat$year - mean(dat$year)

fit <- glm(
  n_students ~ year_c,
  family = poisson,
  data = dat
)

## -------------------------
## Extrapolation grid
## -------------------------
newdat <- data.frame(
  year = seq(1991, 2500, by = 1)
)

newdat$year_c <- newdat$year - mean(dat$year)
newdat$fit <- exp(predict(fit, newdata = newdat, type = "link"))

## -------------------------
## Crossing point (6.3M)
## -------------------------
target <- 6.3e6
idx <- min(which(newdat$fit >= target))

cross_year <- newdat$year[idx]

## -------------------------
## Axis limits
## -------------------------
x_max <- cross_year + 30
y_max <- target * 1.05

## -------------------------
## Plot
## -------------------------
ggplot(newdat, aes(year, fit)) +

  ## model curve
  geom_line(
    colour = "steelblue4",
    linewidth = 0.9
  ) +

  ## observed data
  geom_point(
    data = dat,
    aes(year, n_students),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    colour = "black",
    size = 2
  ) +

  ## horizontal threshold
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = target,
    colour = "firebrick3",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +

  ## vertical crossing line
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = cross_year,
    colour = "firebrick3",
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +

  ## x-axis shows special label "2346"
scale_x_continuous(
  breaks = c(1990, cross_year),
  labels = c("1990", "2346")
)+

  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(1991, x_max),
    ylim = c(0, y_max)
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "Expected number of students"
  ) +

  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +

  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(colour = "grey88"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(colour = "black"),
    plot.margin = margin(8, 12, 8, 8)
  )
```

# Conclusion

* Jonathan will hit the 6.3 million students per year target, but won't be able to retire until 2346. 

\pause

* Despite my efforts to prove otherwise, it's probably not fair to call Jonathan an under-achiever.

# On Behalf of All of Us...

\pause

You will forever be our Sheriff \begingroup\color[RGB]{255,215,0}\faStar\endgroup

```{r, echo=FALSE,fig.align = 'center',  out.width="50%"}
knitr::include_graphics("pics/jpic3.JPG")
```
